zh.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala是Jacopino Della Scala和Elisabeth Superbi的兒子，出生在義大利隆巴迪亞曼托瓦。他於1263年與Verde di Salizzole在義大利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1301年9月3日在義大利維圖維洛納維洛納，埋葬在義大利維圖維洛納維洛納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala是Jacopino Della Scala和Elisabeth Superbi的儿子，出生在意大利隆巴迪亞曼托瓦。他于1263年与Verde di Salizzole在意大利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1301年9月3日在意大利維圖維洛納維洛納，埋葬在意大利維圖維洛納維洛納。

nameMeanings.name: Della
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian:
from the feminine form of   Zello  .
alternatively, perhaps a variant of   Cella  .

variants.name: Zello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Rocco.
Italian:
from a short form of a medieval personal name ending with the hypocoristic suffix -zello, such as for example Donzello.

nameMeanings.name: Scala
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Luigi, Sal, Antonio, Carmine, Corrado, Orazio, Tiziana, Aldo, Angelo, Carlo, Carmela, Concetta.

Italian: topographic name from scala ‘ladder, flight of steps; wharf’ for someone who lived by a flight of steps or on terraced land; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Scala in Salerno province, Scala Coeli in Cosenza, or the district so named in Torregrotta, in Messina province, Sicily. Compare Di Scala and La Scala .
Americanized form of Czech Skála, Polish and Jewish (from Poland) Skała, or Slovak and Rusyn Skala .

variants.name: Di Scala
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Emidio, Luigi, Nicola, Paolo.
Italian: habitational name for someone from any of various places places named with scala ‘ladder, flight of steps; wharf’ (see Scala ).

variants.name: Scalese
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Gennaro, Guido, Pasquale, Vincenzo.
Italian (southern): probably a habitational name for someone from Scala in Salerno province (see   Scala  ).

variants.name: Scalera
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Vito, Angelo, Carmine, Ciro, Gilda, Lorenzo, Luciano, Pasquale.
Italian:
habitational name from a district so named in Filiano in Potenza province.

variants.name: La Scala
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Faust, Natale, Nicola, Silvio.
Italian (also Lascala): variant of Scala , with the addition of the definite article la.

variants.name: Skala
meanings: Czech (Skála); Slovak (also Skála); Polish and Jewish (from Poland) (Skała); Rusyn (from Slovakia and Poland; in Poland spelled Skała): topographic name for someone who lived by a rock or crag, Czech skála, Slovak and Rusyn skala, Polish skała. As a Jewish surname this is often artificial. Compare Scala and Skalla .

variants.name: Scali
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Domenic, Antonio, Geraldo, Orlando, Pasquale, Rocco, Carmela, Cesare, Fortunato, Giuseppe, Mauro, Pasqualina, Piero, Rosario, Saverio.
Italian (southern):
habitational name from Scali in Piedimonte Etneo, Sicily, named with Greek skali(n) ‘step, terrace’ (itself derived from Latin scala ‘ladder’), or from some other similarly named place.

nameMeanings.name: Alberto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: See Albert and Albrecht .

en.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala was born in Mantova, Lombardy, Italy as the son of Jacopino Della Scala and Elisabeth Superbi. He married Verde di Salizzole in 1263, in Italy. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 3 September 1301, in Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italy, and was buried in Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala est né à Mantova, Lombardia, Italie, fils de Jacopino Della Scala et Elisabeth Superbi. Il a épousé Verde di Salizzole en 1263, à Italie. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 3 septembre 1301, à Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italie, et a été enterré à Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italie.

de.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala wurde in Mantova, Lombardei, Italien, als Sohn von Jacopino Della Scala und Elisabeth Superbi geboren. Er hat 1263 in Italien Verde di Salizzole geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 3. September 1301 in Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italien und wurde in Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala è nato il nel Mantova, Lombardia, Italia come figlio di Jacopino Della Scala e Elisabeth Superbi. Lui sposato Verde di Salizzole nel 1263, nel Italia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 3 settembre 1301, nel Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italia, e fu sepolto a Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italia.

ja.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della ScalaはイタリアロンバーディアマントヴァでJacopino Della ScalaとElisabeth Superbiの息子として生まれた。1263年にイタリアでVerde di Salizzoleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1301年9月3日にイタリアベンエトーバーオナバーオナで死去し，イタリアベンエトーバーオナバーオナにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala(은)는 이탈리아 롬바디아 먼토바에서 Jacopino Della Scala(와)과 Elisabeth Superbi의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1263년 이탈리아에서 Verde di Salizzole(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1301년 9월 3일 이탈리아 븐토 베로나 베로나에서 사망했으며 이탈리아 븐토 베로나 베로나에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala nasceu em Mantova, Lombardia, Itália filho de Jacopino Della Scala e Elisabeth Superbi. Ele casou-se com Verde di Salizzole em 1263, em Itália. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 3 de setembro de 1301, em Verona, Verona, Veneto, Itália, e foi sepultado em Verona, Verona, Veneto, Itália.

ru.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala родился в местности мантова, ломбардя, Италия. Его родителей звали Jacopino Della Scala и Elisabeth Superbi. Он женился на женщине по имени Verde di Salizzole в 1263 г. в местности Италия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 3 сентября 1301 г. в местности вирона, вирона, винето, Италия и был похоронен в местности вирона, вирона, винето, Италия.

es.lifeSummary: Alberto I Della Scala nació en Mantova, Lombardia, Italia como hijo de Jacopino Della Scala y Elisabeth Superbi. Se casó con Verde di Salizzole en 1263, en Italia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 3 de septiembre de 1301, en Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italia, y fue sepultado en Verona, Verona, Veneto, Italia.
